pattern: Enumerate every rhombus by its center and half-diagonal, sum its four diagonal borders (or a single cell for size zero), and keep the three largest distinct totals.
hints: A rhombus is fixed by its top corner cell and its half-size; the degenerate size-zero rhombus is just one cell.
For each candidate center and radius, walk the four diagonal edges to add up the border cells, taking care not to double-count the four corner cells.
Collect every valid rhombus sum into a set for distinctness, then return the three largest in descending order (or fewer if there are not three).

edge_inputs.description: five by five from the statement
single row only size-zero rhombi all equal
two by two has no nontrivial rhombus

stress.description: random grids up to moderate size over a small value range to force ties and distinctness handling
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, min(30, max(1, size)))
    n = rng.randint(1, min(30, max(1, size)))
    return [[[rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]]
